upnp.title: UPnP
upnp.desc: When enabled, UPnP allows LAN devices to automatically create temporary WAN port mappings. Active mappings are removed when the client releases them or the lease expires.
upnp.policy_tips: Allow All permits all LAN clients to request WAN port mappings. Restrict only permits matching clients and ports.
upnp.allow_all_desc: All LAN clients can request any WAN port mapping. Use this only on trusted networks.
upnp.restrict_desc: Only matching clients can request WAN port mappings within the allowed port ranges.
upnp.client_error: Please enter a valid IPv4 address or CIDR subnet.
upnp.risk_desc: UPnP can make network services easier to reach, but it also allows LAN clients to ask the router to open ports automatically.
upnp.risk_item_any_device: Any device or app on your LAN can request port mappings without per-request approval.
upnp.risk_item_compromised: If a compromised device, phishing payload, or malicious ad gains control inside your LAN, it may expose internal services to the public Internet.
upnp.risk_item_leak: Some UPnP implementations may leak device or service data across the local network.
upnp.risk_item_trusted: Enable UPnP only on trusted networks and disable it when it is not needed.
upnp.risk_confirm: I understand these risks and still want to enable UPnP.
upnp.delete_mapping_tips: Are you sure you want to delete this automatic port mapping?
